summary.title: New Threat Model test model
summary.description: testa och see funktionaliteten för Threat dragon tool

threats.title: Prompt injection
threats.description: A malicious user crafts prompts to override instructions, reveal hidden prompts, or manipulate model behavior.
threats.mitigation: Input validation, prompt isolation, system prompt hardening, output filtering, adversarial testing

threats.title: Unauthorized API access
threats.description: An attacker bypasses the frontend and sends requests directly to backend APIs without proper authorization.
threats.mitigation: Strong authentication, token validation, authorization checks, rate limiting

threats.title: Indirect prompt injection
threats.description: Malicious text stored in retrieved documents influences the final prompt and changes the model’s behavior.
threats.mitigation: Document sanitization, trust scoring for sources, retrieval filtering, context labeling, prompt segmentation

threats.title: Cross-user data exposure
threats.description: A user accesses another user’s chat history, outcomes, or retrieved context due to broken isolation.
threats.mitigation: Session validation, tenant isolation, access controls, scoped retrieval

threats.title: Vector database poisoning
threats.description: Attackers insert false or malicious knowledge into the vector database so retrieval returns unsafe context.
threats.mitigation: Controlled ingestion, document validation, source verification, review workflow

threats.title: Denial of service through prompt flooding
threats.description: Attackers send many requests or extremely large prompts to consume compute and degrade service.
threats.mitigation: Rate limiting, token limits, quotas, timeout controls

threats.title: Prompt security filter bypass
threats.description: Carefully crafted prompts avoid detection and still reach the model with malicious instructions.
threats.mitigation: Layered filtering, semantic detection, jailbreak testing, fallback blocking
